version: AMOS Macro Regime & Liquidity State Machine v0.1
purpose: Turn macro from commentary into a state machine that constrains portfolio beta, timing, and narrative risk.
core_principles: Macro is a constraint layer, not a single-stock thesis.
The same indicator can mean different things under different regimes.
Market pricing matters more than headline interpretation.
Macro should set beta ceiling, liquidity risk, and event pressure.
AMOS must distinguish growth optimism from valuation compression and fiscal stress.

goldilocks_expansion.description: Growth decent, inflation contained, rates stable or falling, liquidity supportive.

growth_reacceleration.description: Rates rise because growth expectations improve; cyclicals and high-quality growth may both work initially.
growth_reacceleration.risk: can turn into valuation compression if rates move too fast

valuation_compression.description: Rates rise fast enough to pressure long-duration growth valuations.
valuation_compression.portfolio_bias: de-risk high beta, prefer core leaders/cash
valuation_compression.risk: second-line growth breaks before mega-cap leaders

liquidity_expansion.description: System liquidity improves and risk appetite broadens.

liquidity_compression.description: Liquidity tightens; speculative assets and high beta suffer.
liquidity_compression.portfolio_bias: reduce beta, protect profits, wait for panic maturity

risk_off_shock.description: Market reprices sudden risk: war, credit event, inflation shock, policy shock.
risk_off_shock.portfolio_bias: defensive, no aggressive dip-buy until maturity

panic_maturity.description: Forced selling begins to exhaust while core thesis remains intact.
signals: leaders stop going down on bad news
panic_maturity.risk: mistaking early crash for mature panic

euphoria_meltup.description: Liquidity, narrative, gamma, and crowding create upside overshoot.
euphoria_meltup.portfolio_bias: hold leaders, trim extended positions, sell calls selectively

defensive_rotation.description: Capital leaves high beta and moves to cash, bonds, gold, healthcare, utilities or low beta quality.
defensive_rotation.portfolio_bias: reduce high beta; keep only best core
defensive_rotation.risk: holding weak second-line stocks too long

ten_year_yield.rising_good: If earnings revisions improve, credit calm, breadth expanding, and growth sectors still strong.
ten_year_yield.rising_bad: If MOVE rises, DXY strong, high-multiple stocks sell off, credit widens, breadth weakens.
ten_year_yield.falling_good: If rates fall with inflation cooling and risk assets broaden.
ten_year_yield.falling_bad: If rates fall because growth shock or recession fear.

btc.risk_on: BTC rises with growth/high beta and credit calm.
btc.liquidation_warning: BTC falls sharply with leverage liquidation and crypto equities underperform.
btc.divergent_signal: BTC strong while equities weak may indicate crypto-specific flow, not broad risk-on.

dxy.risk_pressure: DXY rising with rates and VIX can pressure global liquidity and growth stocks.
dxy.safe_haven: DXY rising during geopolitical shock may indicate risk-off.

vix_vvix_move.vol_of_vol: VVIX warns about convexity and hedging demand.
vix_vvix_move.rate_vol: MOVE rising can pressure duration assets even if VIX calm.

breadth.healthy: Leaders rising with breadth expansion supports sustainable rally.
breadth.narrow: Index rising on few leaders increases fragility.
breadth.capitulation: Extreme weak breadth may support panic maturity only if forced selling exhausts.

crisis_beta.notes: No aggressive dip buying until panic maturity.

forbidden: assuming BTC down is always broad risk-off
